summary: Technical and on-chain signals are computed triggers — moving-average crosses, leverage/funding extremes, and valuation ratios — that traders watch to time crypto entries and exits. The most backtestable patterns split into two families: lagging trend signals (death/golden cross, 200-day MA loss/reclaim) that confirm moves already underway, and positioning/valuation extremes (funding spikes, open-interest washouts, MVRV Z-score, realized price) that mark exhaustion and tend to mean-revert. In practice the cleanest edges come from forced-liquidation washouts (sharp down, fast bounce) and on-chain undervaluation (MVRV < 1 = multi-month accumulation zone), while MA crosses mostly confirm rather than predict.

archetypes.name: Moving-average regime cross (golden/death cross, 200-DMA loss/reclaim)
archetypes.mechanism: The 50/200-day cross and the 200-day MA are lagging trend filters. They do not cause moves; they confirm a regime that price already entered. The 'other side' is momentum/CTA-style systematic flows that flip exposure on the cross, plus retail that treats the cross as a headline-driven signal. Because the 200-DMA is a widely-watched support/resistance line, it becomes a self-fulfilling cluster of stops and limit orders: losing it triggers stop cascades; reclaiming it triggers breakout buying. The recurring loser is the trader who treats the death cross as a fresh short entry near the local low — historically returns in the first ~3 weeks after a BTC death cross are roughly a coin flip and 2-3 months later average a 15-26% recovery.
archetypes.typical_lag: lagging — the cross prints days-to-weeks AFTER the price move; the 200-DMA break itself reacts within hours
archetypes.typical_duration: regime label persists weeks to months; the acute stop-cascade on a 200-DMA loss plays out in 1-3 days
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: 200-DMA loss often accompanies a -10% to -25% drawdown already in progress; reclaim tends to precede +15% to +40% over 1-3 months
archetypes.recurrence: BTC has printed multiple death crosses 2018-2025; 200-DMA tested multiple times per cycle
leading_signals: price closing below 200-DMA on rising volume

historical_instances.trigger: BTC 50-DMA crossed below 200-DMA (death cross) ~6 weeks after the Oct 6 ATH near $126k; price had already fallen ~25% and broke below the 200-DMA region; coincided with record IBIT/ETF outflows (~$1.26B mid-Nov).
historical_instances.description: Confirmed death cross arriving well after the top — a textbook lagging signal that printed as BTC continued lower toward $82k.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: BlackRock IBIT ~$1.26B net outflows mid-November; record cumulative BTC ETF withdrawals.

historical_instances.trigger: BTC closed below its 200-day SMA (~$39,825) for the first time since 29 Apr 2020, breaking a key watched support and triggering stop cascades; Musk/Tesla BTC-payment reversal added sentiment shock.
historical_instances.description: Classic 200-DMA loss: the break of the widely-watched line accelerated a forced-liquidation crash and confirmed the cycle had topped. Intraday low ~$28,758 (max drawdown ~33.9% from the day's open).

price_impacts.window: intraday 19 May 2021 (open ~$43k to low ~$28.8k; ~33.9% max drawdown)

historical_instances.trigger: BTC formed a daily golden cross (50-DMA above 200-DMA) in late February 2024 as the spot-ETF-driven rally accelerated.
historical_instances.description: Golden cross confirming an uptrend that then carried BTC to a new ATH (~$74k) in mid-March 2024 — illustrates the cross as trend confirmation, not entry timing.

historical_instances.trigger: BTC daily golden cross (50-DMA above 200-DMA) coming out of the March 2020 COVID crash.
historical_instances.description: Golden cross preceding the 2020-2021 bull run; confirmed recovery from the COVID liquidation low.

historical_instances.trigger: BTC death cross printed in September 2023 near a local low of the 2023 chop.
historical_instances.description: Counter-example showing the death cross firing as a contrarian bottom marker rather than a continuation short — weakness was short-lived, a golden cross followed in October 2023, and price ultimately rallied toward the $74k March 2024 ATH.

archetypes.name: Open-interest washout / forced-liquidation cascade
archetypes.mechanism: When perpetual-futures open interest and leverage build up (often alongside elevated funding), a sharp price move triggers margin calls. Exchange liquidation engines market-sell (or buy) collateral, pushing price further into the same direction, triggering the next tier of liquidations — a reflexive deleveraging cascade. The losers are the over-leveraged longs (in a down-washout) whose stops/liquidations become the fuel; the winners are spot buyers and patient capital who absorb the forced selling at a discount. Because the move is mechanical (positions, not fundamentals), it typically overshoots and snaps back fast — the washout itself is often a short-term bottom.
archetypes.typical_lag: immediate — minutes to hours; the full cascade often completes inside 24h
archetypes.typical_duration: acute crash 1-2 days; OI/leverage reset persists weeks; price often recovers a large fraction within 1-2 weeks
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: BTC -8% to -30% in 24h; alts/high-beta -15% to -50%; bounce of +5% to +15% within days
archetypes.recurrence: the largest cascades cluster ~1-2 per year, with the biggest in May 2021, Aug 2024, and Oct 2025
leading_signals: aggregate open interest at cycle highs
thin spot order-book depth into a macro catalyst

historical_instances.trigger: Largest single-day crypto liquidation in history (~$19B+ across exchanges) — a leverage washout amplified by thin liquidity; high-beta alts hit hardest.
historical_instances.description: Record forced-liquidation cascade: BTC, ETH and SOL gapped down within 24h then partially recovered, a classic deleveraging overshoot.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$19B+ total liquidations in ~24h — the largest on record (multiple sources cite >$19B).

historical_instances.trigger: Leverage washout as BTC broke the 200-DMA; >$8B liquidations across the market.
historical_instances.description: One of the largest cascades of the cycle; >$8B liquidated and BTC plunged to an intraday low of ~$28,758 (~34% max drawdown) before a sharp rebound — a deleveraging overshoot.

historical_instances.trigger: Yen carry-trade unwind (BOJ rate hike) margin-called risk assets globally; crypto leverage washed out with ~$1B liquidations.
historical_instances.description: Macro-triggered forced deleveraging: BTC and ETH each fell ~20-25% intraday/over 1-2 days; worst crypto futures day in months.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: ~$1B crypto-futures liquidations in 24h (>275,000 traders); Nikkei -12%, Nasdaq -6%.

historical_instances.trigger: FTX collapse / contagion forced deleveraging; funding turned deeply negative.
historical_instances.description: Solvency-driven cascade that marked the cycle bottom; BTC fell from ~$21k to a ~$15.5k low (~26%), and deeply negative funding flagged the panic low before a multi-month recovery.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Funding deeply negative; OI and leverage reset to cycle lows.

archetypes.name: On-chain valuation extreme (MVRV Z-score, realized price, NUPL, Pi-cycle)
archetypes.mechanism: These metrics compare market price to aggregate cost basis (realized value). MVRV Z-score, realized price, NUPL and the Pi-cycle top indicator all measure how far price has stretched from what holders actually paid. At extreme highs (MVRV-Z > ~7, NUPL in euphoria, Pi-cycle crossover) holders are sitting on huge unrealized gains and the marginal buyer is exhausted — the other side is profit-taking long-term holders distributing into euphoric retail. At extreme lows (MVRV ratio < 1, price below realized price) the average holder is underwater — historically a maximum-pain accumulation zone where patient capital buys from capitulating sellers. Mean-reverting and slow: these are cycle markers, not day-trade triggers.
archetypes.typical_lag: slow — signals fire days-to-weeks around tops/bottoms; full reversion plays out over months
archetypes.typical_duration: months; sub-1.0 MVRV windows lasted ~15 days (Mar 2020), ~178 days (2022), ~305 days (2018-19)
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: buying sub-1.0 MVRV historically returned triple-digit % within 12 months; selling MVRV-Z>7 avoided 50-80% cycle drawdowns
archetypes.recurrence: once or twice per ~4-year cycle for the full extreme
leading_signals: MVRV Z-score crossing above 7 (top) or MVRV ratio below 1 (bottom)

historical_instances.trigger: MVRV Z-score spiked to roughly ~7 at the April 2021 local top.
historical_instances.description: Extreme overvaluation reading at the first 2021 peak; the lower Z-score at the November higher price formed a bearish divergence warning the cycle was maturing.

historical_instances.trigger: Cycle ATH ~$69k with MVRV-Z making a LOWER high than April (bearish divergence) — realized cap had caught up to price.
historical_instances.description: On-chain valuation flagged the final top via divergence; preceded the 2022 bear market.

historical_instances.trigger: MVRV ratio fell below 1.0 (price below average cost basis / realized price) during the 2022 bear; ~178 days sub-1.0 in that cycle.
historical_instances.description: Maximum-pain accumulation zone — buying the sub-1.0 MVRV window preceded triple-digit returns within 12 months.

historical_instances.trigger: MVRV ratio dipped below 1.0 for ~15 days during the COVID crash.
historical_instances.description: Brief but extreme undervaluation at the COVID low; accumulators in the sub-1.0 window earned triple-digit returns within 12 months.

historical_instances.trigger: MVRV Z-score reached only ~3-4 at the $100k+ ATH — elevated but well below historical >7 extremes.
historical_instances.description: ETF-driven cycle showed a muted valuation profile: the metric never reached classic euphoria, signalling a structurally different, institution-led regime rather than an imminent blow-off top.

archetypes.name: Funding-rate / leverage-sentiment extreme (contrarian)
archetypes.mechanism: Perpetual funding is paid from the crowded side to the other side. Extreme positive funding (e.g. 0.2-0.3% per 8h sustained for weeks) means longs are paying heavily to stay long — crowded, over-leveraged positioning that signals euphoria and a vulnerable market near local tops. Deeply negative funding means shorts are paying — capitulation/panic positioning that often marks bottoms. The recurring losers are momentum-chasers piling into the crowded side at the extreme; the winners are contrarians who fade the crowd and/or harvest the funding (cash-and-carry). Funding extremes are a sentiment gauge, not precise timing — they identify fragility, with the actual reversal often needing a catalyst (which then becomes an OI washout).
archetypes.typical_lag: days to weeks — extreme funding flags fragility; the reversal follows on a catalyst
archetypes.typical_duration: the positioning unwind resolves over days once triggered; funding normalizes within a week of a washout
archetypes.typical_magnitude_pct: fading sustained extreme-positive funding historically preceded -20% to -50% cycle corrections; buying deeply-negative funding preceded +30% to +600% recoveries
archetypes.recurrence: milder funding spikes several times a year; true multi-week extremes once or twice a cycle
leading_signals: funding rate sustained >0.1-0.3% per 8h for days/weeks (top warning)
perp basis / annualized basis at extreme

historical_instances.trigger: Perpetual funding (e.g. BitMEX XBTUSD) ran elevated, around 0.2-0.3% per 8h at times, into the April 2021 first peak.
historical_instances.description: Sustained elevated-positive funding flagged euphoric over-leveraged longs ahead of the May 2021 crash — a textbook 'too crowded long' top warning, though exact sustained levels vary by source.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Funding elevated near 0.2-0.3%/8h at peaks; OI at cycle highs.

historical_instances.trigger: Funding flipped to deep-negative values unseen for months during the May 19 crash.
historical_instances.description: Negative-funding extreme at the panic acted as a leading indicator for the subsequent short-term rebound off the ~$30k area.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Funding deeply negative; >$8B liquidations cleared leverage.

historical_instances.trigger: Funding turned deeply negative during the FTX-collapse cascade.
historical_instances.description: Deep-negative funding at the cycle bottom (~$15.5k) preceded a multi-hundred-percent recovery to new ATH — a high-conviction contrarian buy signal in hindsight.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Funding deeply negative; leverage reset to cycle lows.

historical_instances.trigger: BitMEX max funding only ~0.13% in 2024 even as BTC made new highs; sustained high rates effectively non-existent.
historical_instances.description: Notable NON-signal: the 2024 ETF-led top lacked the euphoric funding spike of prior cycles, meaning the classic crowded-long warning never fully triggered — a regime-shift caveat for the backtest.
historical_instances.volume_or_flow_change: Funding muted vs prior cycles despite new ATHs.
